creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: WF2026: Software Factories & Keynotes ft. Microsoft, OpenAI, OpenClaw, Z.ai (GLM), MiniMax, HF
videos.summary: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 convenes major AI companies at Moscone West in San Francisco on June 30, 2026, featuring keynotes from Microsoft, OpenAI, OpenClaw, Z.ai (GLM), MiniMax, and Hugging Face. The event highlights software factories as a central theme in AI engineering and industry practice.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: Matt Pocock presents the Skill Checklist Framework for building high-quality AI agent skills, addressing "skill hell" through structured guidance on triggering (user vs model-invoked), organization (steps and references), steering (leading words for agent guidance), and pruning (removing legacy material and no-ops). The framework emphasizes maintainability, clarity, and intentional agent behavior through minimal skill.md files and context pointers.

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors presents a framework for deciding when small local models can replace frontier models, covering capability evaluations, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge approaches. The talk includes production-trace-based eval construction and iterative prompt optimization using real-world examples like agentic harnesses and content moderation classifiers.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Domain-specific agents composed together offer a more powerful alternative to monolithic AI systems. Smaller models become capable of real work when agents for tools like Gmail, Sheets, Notion, and GitHub are composed together, enabling customer-facing AI products while reducing costs and improving reliability.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Released Claude Sonnet 5 and it's VERY IMPRESSIVE!
videos.summary: Cole Medin provides a positive reaction to Anthropic's release of Claude Sonnet 5, highlighting it as an impressive model for the cost. The video frames Sonnet 5 within a broader discussion of AI's transformative potential and the creator's focus on mastering AI agents.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Google's whitepaper argues that "vibe coding" does not scale, and the model choice contributes only 10% to agentic engineering outcomes while the "harness" (context, tools, guardrails, verification) contributes 90%. The video breaks down the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, context engineering as a key skill, and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a cross-agent memory layer that stores encrypted user-owned context outside individual AI tools. Users can share memory between agents from different providers like Claude and Pi with fine-grained access control and revocation capabilities.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The reviewer notes it is impressive for its price and size.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: I was giving my coding agent context the wrong way...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses best practices for giving coding agents context, suggesting there is a wrong way and implying a correct approach. The video references a codebase harness tool and positions context management as an engineering concern for agent systems.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: OpenClaw Creator announces a new project involving Crabbox and Codebase harness tools available via GitHub. The initiative includes an AI Builder Club membership for step-by-step walkthroughs and a Superdesign product offering.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer, a framework for building AI agents. The video includes setup instructions, a GitHub repository with code templates, and a workshop through the AI Builder Club.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive agents that create self-improving company systems. It covers the architecture of AI loops, memory mechanisms, and tool integration, with applied examples in autonomous ad optimization and SEO automation.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses OpenAI's announcement of GPT 5.6 and its regulatory oversight as the AI industry matures. The video compares this with Anthropic's paused Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models, and notes OpenAI's consolidation of models into three offerings: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining how transformers work under the hood, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The content walks through fundamental components that power models like Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model tutorial, covering tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and optimization. The video serves as foundational material for understanding transformer-based models and GPT architectures.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series of models in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and MTP techniques for improved performance.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Containers on Amazon ECS with Mama J
videos.summary: AWS Developers video explaining containerization and container orchestration using Amazon ECS. Covers the problem containers solve, Docker basics, ECS as an orchestrator, Fargate vs EC2 deployment options, and practical example with auto-scaling.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a 7-step system design framework: requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach emphasizes problem-first design rather than starting with technology choices. Demonstrated through a URL shortener interview example.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling is a technique where AI agents request external tools to overcome LLM limitations such as stale training data, lack of file access, and inability to take actions. The process involves the model requesting a tool, the framework executing it, and the loop repeating with results. Tool calling requires schema definition, description, and handler implementation with system prompt guidance on tool selection.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS enables AI coding agents to interact with AWS services by providing up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It improves agent success rates and reduces errors compared to relying on general knowledge alone.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Why is AI expensive all of a sudden?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines the rising costs of AI systems, tracing the phenomenon through automation cycles and the trend of "tokenmaxxing" where developers artificially increase token usage to boost revenue. The video ultimately identifies deeper structural reasons for AI expense beyond token inflation tricks.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video examining whether Google engineers use code generation tools, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, and speculating on code generation adoption at Google across 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source, discussing its implications for open source maintainers and the ecosystem. The video frames the topic as a provocative debate about whether Malus represents an existential threat to open source.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: He Built $100B at Google, Then Failed. Now He Runs an $80B+ Company | Snowflake, Sridhar Ramaswamy
videos.summary: Sridhar Ramaswamy, CEO of Snowflake, discusses his career trajectory from building Google's search business to founding the failed Neeva search engine and lessons learned. He shares principles for leading companies into the AI era, emphasizing resilience after failure and the importance of staying close to product development.

videos.title: Everything You Should Know Before Mythos Arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke, founder of Nebulock, argues that AI has dramatically reduced the cost and complexity of launching cyberattacks, collapsing what once required elite teams and years into work for two people with a GPU. He advocates an "assume breach" defensive posture and outlines three behavioral signals that reveal attackers already inside networks, emphasizing that defenders must shift from reactive alerting to proactive threat hunting.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software advantage comes from customer context, proprietary data, and product experience rather than code alone. He contends that AI will not disrupt SaaS as dramatically as feared and that the best AI products create raving fans rather than merely satisfied customers.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a humanoid robot race led by companies like 1X and Unitree represents a narrow vision of robotics' future. She contends that designing robots for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter society.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI vs. Dedicated Software: The Real Efficiency Debate #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof questions whether integrating AI broadly for every task delivers long-term efficiency gains or constitutes wasteful spending. The piece challenges the assumption that AI universally outperforms dedicated software solutions and suggests drawbacks to blanket AI adoption.

videos.title: Germany Heatwave MELTED my Ice Cream in Lisbon! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube shorts post describes extreme heat affecting German and Portuguese cities, with ice cream melting as an illustrative detail. The post attributes the intensified conditions to the urban heat island effect and climate change.

videos.title: AI Learns, Takes Initiative, Works Asynchronously: The Future is Here! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof presents a vision of AI systems that learn continuously, operate asynchronously, and take initiative autonomously. The piece emphasizes organizational learning and ambient mode operation as defining characteristics of the near future.

videos.title: Claude Tag: AI's European Takeover in Microsoft Teams! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof explores the strategic integration of Claude Tag into Microsoft Teams and Slack, examining whether this represents a deliberate enterprise adoption strategy by Anthropic. The content frames Claude Tag as an AI integration play targeting European markets and enterprise SaaS adoption.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
